options.A: above the average total cost curve

options.D: above the demand curve

text: Which of the following is a necessary condition for price discrimination?

options.A: Resale of the product or service is possible.

text: Which of the following would cause the wage for computer programmers to increase?

options.A: A decrease in the amount of physical capital per computer programmer
options.B: An improvement in the education of computer programmers
options.C: An increase in the social prestige associated with being a computer programmer
options.D: A decrease in the value that computer programmers place on leisure

text: Command economies differ from capitalist economies mainly because of which of the following?

options.C: Command economies have higher degree of government ownership of resources.
options.D: Capitalist economies have higher degree of government ownership of resources.
options.E: Command economies rely on prices to allocate resources.

options.C: It would have no impact on the number of housing units available.
options.D: It would increase the consumer surplus and decrease the producer surplus.
options.E: It would decrease the consumer surplus and increase the producer surplus.

text: A firm is currently employing a cost-minimizing combination of labor and capital for a given level of output. The firm is employing 20 workers, and the marginal product of the last worker is 40 units of output. The firm is employing 100 units of capital, and the marginal product of the last unit of capital is 10 units of output. If the wage rate is $20 per hour, what is the price of capital?

options.A: $2.5 per hour
options.B: $5 per hour
options.C: $10 per hour
options.D: $100 per hour
options.E: $200 per hour

text: If a firm's production function exhibits diminishing marginal product of the variable input in the short run, which of the following about the firm's short-run marginal cost (MC) curve must be true?

options.A: As output increases, the MC curve slopes upward.
options.B: As output increases, the MC curve slopes downward and becomes flatter.
options.C: As output increases, the MC curve slopes downward and becomes steeper.
options.D: The MC curve is horizontal.
options.E: The MC curve is vertical.

text: Catering, Inc., which provides catering services in a perfectly competitive market, was maximizing profits at the market price of $22 per meal. The market price has recently increased to $28 per meal. Which of the following short-run adjustments will increase profits for Catering, Inc.?

options.A: Increasing output until the marginal cost equals the new price
options.B: Increasing output until the average total cost equals the new price
options.C: Decreasing output because of the increase in market price
options.D: Increasing the wage rate paid to its workers
options.E: Increasing advertising to increase demand for the meals any part of this page is illegal.

options.C: excessive competition prevents other firms from entering the market
options.D: homogeneous goods are usually overpriced
options.E: short-run economic profit-making opportunities exist

text: A profit-maximizing, perfectly competitive firm is currently in long-run equilibrium. It is earning $15,000 of total revenue from a sale of 1,000 units. Its total fixed cost of production is $2,500. Which of the following can correctly be inferred from the information provided?

options.D: Its marginal cost is $15.00, and its average variable cost is $12.50.
options.E: Its marginal cost is $15.00, and its average fixed cost is $12.50.

text: Both Amy and Brad produce and consume apple pie and ice cream. In one hour, Brad makes five apple pies or ten gallons of ice cream while Amy makes fifteen apple pies or fifteen gallons of ice cream. Based on the above information, one can correctly conclude that

options.A: Brad has an absolute advantage in making ice cream and will sell ice cream to Amy
options.B: Brad has a comparative advantage in making ice cream and will sell ice cream to Amy
options.C: Brad does not have a comparative advantage in making either good and will not trade with Amy
options.D: Brad has an absolute advantage in making apple pies but a comparative advantage in making ice cream
options.E: Brad's opportunity cost of making ice cream is higher than Amy's

text: In a monopsonistic labor market, a firm will continue to hire workers until the marginal revenue product of labor

options.A: equals marginal factor (resource) cost, resulting in more workers being hired at a lower wage compared with a perfectly competitive labor market
options.B: equals marginal factor (resource) cost, resulting in fewer workers being hired at a lower wage compared with a perfectly competitive labor market
options.C: equals marginal factor (resource) cost, resulting in fewer workers being hired at a higher wage compared with a perfectly competitive labor market
options.D: is less than marginal factor (resource) cost, resulting in more workers being hired at a lower wage compared with a perfectly competitive labor market

text: Assume that a monopolistically competitive firm is currently maximizing profit with an output of 100 units and a price of $50. Which of the following is true?

options.A: Total revenue is maximized when the firm produces 100 units of output.
options.B: Marginal revenue equals $50 when the firm produces 100 units of output.
options.C: Marginal cost is greater than marginal revenue when the firm produces 150 units of output.
options.D: Marginal cost is minimized when the firm produces 100 units of output.

text: There are four firms in an oligopolistic industry. The four firms agree to collude and act like a monopoly. If one of the firms violates the agreement and charges a lower price or sells a larger quantity than what was agreed to, what will happen in the short run?

options.A: The firm that cheats will earn higher profits, and industry profits will be lower.
options.B: The firm that cheats will earn higher profits, and industry profits will be higher.
options.C: The firm that cheats will earn lower profits, and industry profits will be lower.
options.D: The firm that cheats will earn lower profits, and industry profits will be higher.
options.E: The firms that do not cheat will earn higher profits.

text: Mickey has a $20 gift card that she is planning to use to download 10 songs and 5 games. The download price for each song is $1 and the download price for each game is $2. Her marginal utility from the tenth song is 30, and her marginal utility from the fifth game is 70. Based on this information, which of the following statements is true?

options.A: Mickey's plan will maximize her utility.
options.B: Mickey can increase utility by buying more songs and fewer games.
options.C: Mickey can increase utility by buying more games and fewer songs.
options.D: Since games are twice as expensive as songs, Mickey should buy twice as many songs.
options.E: Since games are twice as expensive as songs, Mickey should buy twice as many games.

text: Which of the following is true of a monopolistically competitive firm in long-run equilibrium?

options.A: It produces where price equals marginal cost, and it earns zero economic profits.
options.B: It produces where marginal revenue exceeds marginal cost, and it earns positive economic profits.
options.C: It produces where marginal cost equals marginal revenue, and the price is equal to average total cost.
options.D: It produces at the minimum average total cost, and it utilizes all excess capacity.
options.E: It produces more than the allocatively efficient quantity, and the price is greater than marginal cost.

text: Assume that barber shops operate in perfectly competitive product and factor markets. Which of the following will happen to working barbers if the price of haircuts decreases?

options.A: The marginal product of the last barber hired will not change.
options.B: The marginal product of the last barber hired will decrease.
options.C: The marginal revenue product curve will shift to the right.
options.D: The marginal revenue product curve will shift to the left.
options.E: There will be a movement upward along the marginal revenue curve.

text: In a market with two firms, a firm that has a dominant strategy will do which of the following?

options.A: Maintain that strategy independent of the strategies chosen by its competitor.
options.B: Adjust its strategies based on the strategies chosen by its competitor.
options.C: Make the first move, and wait to see whether its competitor responds to the move.
options.D: Keep its competitor guessing about its next move.
options.E: Collude with its competitor to reduce uncertainty.

options.B: Monopoly power
options.C: Double taxation of corporations
options.D: Price ceilings
options.E: The provision of public goods

text: If the government regulates a natural monopolist to produce the allocatively efficient level of output, it will require the monopolist to set a price that is

options.A: equal to its marginal cost and grant a subsidy to cover the loss

options.C: greater than its marginal cost and levy a tax on the excess profit
options.D: greater than its marginal cost but that minimizes the deadweight loss
options.E: greater than its average total cost but that minimizes the deadweight loss
